subject: ap-government
title: Concept Application — NEA and Teacher Salaries
stimulus:
According to the National Educational Association (NEA), a national teachers' union with 3.2 million members and an annual budget of over $300 million dollars, the United States is facing a sizable and expanding shortage of teachers. The report indicates that the shortage is especially severe in subjects such as math, science, and special education, where finding qualified teachers to fill positions can be hampered by low starting salaries.

The report finds that the average salary for a teacher is $60,000, but for a new teacher it is usually less than $40,000. Furthermore, salaries adjusted for inflation have declined 4.5% in the past decade. The report also indicates that the teacher shortage is considerably worse in high-poverty areas.

NEA President Lily Eskelsen García has called for higher salaries to address the issue. "How can we recruit and retain quality teachers for our students if we don't pay them what they're worth? It is time to show respect to those professionals who dedicate their lives to students and building the future of our communities. Professional work deserves professional pay."

parts.prompt: Describe a strategy that this interest group can use to address the issue in the scenario.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must describe a specific strategy — not just state that the group could advocate. Accept any plausible interest group strategy appropriate to the NEA's size and resources.

scoring_points.description: Describe a strategy that this interest group can use to address the issue in the scenario.

required_elements: The National Education Association (NEA) can organize political participation (e.g., protests, letter-writing campaigns, canvassing) throughout the country to draw attention to the issue of teachers’ salaries.
alternatives.correct_example: The National Education Association (NEA) can organize political participation (e.g., protests, letter-writing campaigns, canvassing) throughout the country to draw attention to the issue of teachers’ salaries.

required_elements: The NEA can launch a media campaign that supports increasing teachers’ salaries.
alternatives.correct_example: The NEA can launch a media campaign that supports increasing teachers’ salaries.

required_elements: The NEA can lobby lawmakers to increase funding for teachers’ salaries.
alternatives.correct_example: The NEA can lobby lawmakers to increase funding for teachers’ salaries.

required_elements: The NEA can support candidates for Congress that advocate for higher salaries for teachers.
alternatives.correct_example: The NEA can support candidates for Congress that advocate for higher salaries for teachers.

parts.prompt: In the context of the scenario, explain how the resources available to the NEA could impact the effectiveness of the strategy in part A.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must reference specific NEA resources from the scenario (membership size or budget) and explain how they enhance the strategy's effectiveness.

scoring_points.description: In the context of the scenario, explain how the resources available to the NEA could impact the effectiveness of the strategy in part A.

required_elements: The NEA’s resources include a large member base. Having a vast membership at its disposal makes it easier to organize political participation that could effectively address the issue of teachers’ salaries.
alternatives.correct_example: The NEA’s resources include a large member base. Having a vast membership at its disposal makes it easier to organize political participation that could effectively address the issue of teachers’ salaries.

required_elements: The NEA’s resources include an annual budget. To be effective, the NEA could allocate part of its budget to pay for strategies (e.g., influencing lawmakers, launching a social media campaign, buying advertisements) to increase teachers’ salaries.
alternatives.correct_example: The NEA’s resources include an annual budget. To be effective, the NEA could allocate part of its budget to pay for strategies (e.g., influencing lawmakers, launching a social media campaign, buying advertisements) to increase teachers’ salaries.

parts.prompt: Explain how a block grant for education could affect the issue in the scenario in some states but not in others.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must explain both the flexibility feature of block grants AND how that flexibility would create differential outcomes on teacher salaries across states.

scoring_points.description: Explain how a block grant for education could affect the issue in the scenario in some states but not in others.

required_elements: Block grants allow states flexibility in determining how the money is spent. Some states might decide that the block grant funds should be used for educational spending other than teacher salaries. This may create variation in teacher pay between the states. Total for question 1
alternatives.correct_example: Block grants allow states flexibility in determining how the money is spent. Some states might decide that the block grant funds should be used for educational spending other than teacher salaries. This may create variation in teacher pay between the states. Total for question 1
